news.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs

news.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

news.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

news.title: Nvidia Returns To The Bond Market With A $25 Billion Sale
news.description: [FORBES] The chipmaker had not borrowed in the public bond market since 2021. Its first sale in five years tells you how far the cost of building artificial intelligence has now reached into the staid world of investment-grade credit.

news.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI

news.title: AI's thirst trap: tech giant taps into recycled water
news.description: [PERTHNOW] One Australian state is set to get a data centre cooled with recycled water after criticism that the artificial intelligence hubs consume too many resources.

news.title: India’s Schools Need a Revolution, Not Another Language
news.description: [BLOOMBERG] In an era dominated by artificial intelligence, where India is getting left behind, why is Prime Minister Narendra Modi forcing teenagers to learn the ancient language of Sanskrit?

news.description: [U.S. NEWS & WORLD REPORT] WASHINGTON (AP) — As artificial intelligence companies reshape the economy and race toward trillion-dollar valuations, Sen. Bernie Sanders is proposing a sweeping transfer of wealth and power from the industry to the American public.

news.title: ICE Says Detainees Aren't Entitled To Wages
news.description: [HUFFPOST] Contractors running Immigration and Customs Enforcement facilities can rely more heavily on artificial intelligence tools to communicate with detainees and continue refusing to pay wages for detainees’ “voluntary work."

news.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.
